title: leiocarpa serpens
intro: Leiocarpa serpens, also known as snake bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Western Australia, where it grows as a small shrub in the sand plains and woodlands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, skin conditions, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Leiocarpa serpens can be made into teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support overall health and well-being, and to treat a range of health conditions.
